purpose: Define the emotional paths used by Hook Studio Pattern Cards and provide enough intra-mood variance for concept generation and recommendation diversity.

mood_paths.definition: Open with an impending mistake, deadline, safety concern, or fragile moment, then release pressure by showing that the product or product-relevant action restores control.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for products that prevent failure, reduce friction, protect users, or make a stressful task feel manageable.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The product must appear as the believable prevention, stabilizer, or shortcut before the viewer feels tricked by the opening pressure.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s show a near miss; 1-3s introduce product control; 3-5s show the task completed safely.
0-1s deadline or demand appears; 1-3s product/tool handles the load; 3-5s show finished output.
0-1s caregiver notices a problem; 1-3s product resolves the friction; 3-5s child or home state calms.
0-1s show strain or instability; 1-3s product enables control; 3-5s show a stable result.
0-1s call out a common mistake; 1-3s reveal product fix; 3-5s show avoided loss.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Do not exaggerate danger beyond what the product can plausibly solve.

mood_paths.definition: Start with dirt, residue, odor, mess, or texture discomfort, then move toward a visibly cleaner or more pleasant state.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for cleaning, personal care, pet, food handling, and any product with a visible before/after payoff.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The unpleasant cue must create a direct product-relevant need, and the payoff must be visible rather than only claimed.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s reveal a stain; 1-3s product acts; 3-5s show clean contrast.
0-1s macro discomfort; 1-3s treatment or use; 3-5s smooth result.
0-1s reaction to odor; 1-3s product intervention; 3-5s comfortable social state.
0-1s pet mess appears; 1-3s product cleans or contains it; 3-5s home returns to order.
0-1s dirty before; 1-3s action bridge; 3-5s clear after proof.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Avoid disgust cues that overpower the product payoff or make the product category feel unsafe.

mood_paths.definition: Open with social exposure, appearance failure, or self-conscious pressure, then resolve into regained confidence or social readiness.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for beauty, womenswear, personal care, education, and social-performance products.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The product should repair, prepare, or reframe the social moment quickly, without shaming the viewer.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s social exposure; 1-3s product repair; 3-5s confident return.
0-1s outfit issue; 1-3s styling/product fix; 3-5s composed look.
0-1s person names insecurity; 1-3s product use; 3-5s confident proof line.
0-1s gaze creates pressure; 1-3s product changes the state; 3-5s gaze becomes approval.
0-1s common error; 1-3s tool or method correction; 3-5s user appears capable.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Do not make humiliation the memorable element; the confidence turn must dominate by second 5.

mood_paths.definition: Start with clutter, overload, mess, scattered tasks, or sensory chaos, then create a clear order state through product use.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for storage, cleaning, AI tools, education, kitchen tools, and home organization.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The product must be the organizing principle, not just something placed into an already organized scene.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s messy room; 1-3s organizing action; 3-5s ordered space.
0-1s overloaded task list; 1-3s tool groups work; 3-5s clear output.
0-1s family mess; 1-3s product action; 3-5s calmer home state.
0-1s cluttered surface; 1-3s product creates zones; 3-5s neat result.
0-1s confusing claim; 1-3s method/tool organizes it; 3-5s viewer sees a simple plan.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Avoid generic tidy-room imagery without a visible organizing mechanism.

mood_paths.definition: Create an information gap through an unfinished action, hidden cause, strange result, or counterintuitive claim, then reveal the product-relevant answer.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for AI tools, education, beauty, food, product demos, and any offer with a surprising mechanism.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The reveal must answer the curiosity gap with product value rather than only novelty.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s action stops before answer; 1-3s product explains or completes it; 3-5s result appears.
0-1s show odd result; 1-3s reveal product mechanism; 3-5s repeatable proof.
0-1s surprising claim; 1-3s proof action; 3-5s viewer sees why.
0-1s problem without cause; 1-3s reveal cause; 3-5s product resolves it.
0-1s conceal the key state; 1-3s reveal transition; 3-5s product value is clear.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Do not leave the gap unresolved or reveal something unrelated to product value.

mood_paths.definition: Open with interpersonal, household, task, or expectation conflict, then show a product-relevant route to resolution.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for home, mother-baby, services, education, AI tools, and products that mediate friction.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The conflict must be a product-relevant friction, not drama used as a pure attention decoy.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s conflict signal; 1-3s product reframes the task; 3-5s shared resolution.
0-1s parent-child friction; 1-3s product makes the action easier; 3-5s calmer interaction.
0-1s sudden request; 1-3s tool creates answer; 3-5s task conflict resolves.
0-1s visitor pressure; 1-3s product fixes visible issue; 3-5s social moment recovers.
0-1s skeptical comment; 1-3s product proof; 3-5s comment is answered.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Avoid conflict that cannot resolve within 3-5 seconds or that distracts from product proof.

mood_paths.definition: Open with an aspirational identity, lifestyle, taste, or desired self-image, then let the viewer project themselves into the result.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for womenswear, beauty, food, sports fitness, personal care, and lifestyle products.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The product must become the attainable bridge to the projected identity or lifestyle state.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s name the desired identity; 1-3s product shows the route; 3-5s viewer sees themselves in it.
0-1s show desired occasion; 1-3s outfit/product completes it; 3-5s confident scene.
0-1s sensory desire; 1-3s product experience; 3-5s satisfying reaction.
0-1s recognizable style world; 1-3s product joins it; 3-5s viewer imagines belonging.
0-1s desired movement or body state; 1-3s product support; 3-5s attainable result.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Avoid pure fantasy that never shows how the product makes the projected state attainable.

mood_paths.definition: Open with a surprising result, extreme contrast, impossible-looking moment, or strong claim, then immediately prove how it connects to the product.
mood_paths.commerce_role: Best for proof-heavy products, cleaning, beauty, kitchen tools, AI tools, and outcome-oriented demos.
mood_paths.product_bridge_logic: The shock cue must be followed by concrete proof, not only a claim or exaggerated reaction.

variants.example_structure: 0-1s shocking contrast; 1-3s product action; 3-5s repeatable proof.
0-1s compressed outcome; 1-3s show mechanism; 3-5s final result confirms it.
0-1s close proof detail; 1-3s product context; 3-5s full scene verifies.
0-1s audio or rhythm shock; 1-3s product proof starts; 3-5s proof becomes visible.
0-1s surprising social proof; 1-3s product reason; 3-5s concrete result or evidence.

mood_paths.negative_boundary: Avoid shock claims that the generated clip cannot substantiate visually.
